features.description: Pin the iOS app to a light appearance via .preferredColorScheme(.light) at the RootView/WindowGroup root, so every screen, field, and SwiftUI Form renders in the intended light-paper palette regardless of the system Dark Mode setting. This fixes the white-on-white field text (TextField/SecureField previously used SwiftUI's adaptive .primary, which turned white in Dark Mode over the fixed light card backgrounds) and the inverse dark-Form-background problem in one change. Additionally set explicit .foregroundStyle(Theme.ink) on the field text as belt-and-suspenders.
features.notes: Verification: a new XCUITest launches the app under a forced Dark Mode simulator appearance (simctl ui <udid> appearance dark) and asserts the auth fields and add-task field remain present/interactive; existing 20 iOS tests (15 unit + 5 UI) still pass; manual visual sign-off that fields render dark-ink on light. No web files change. Theme.ink (0x333333) already exists (Theme.swift:8), so Theme.swift may need no edit (scope superset).
approaches_tried: First attempt used an -AppleInterfaceStyle Dark launch argument to force the app dark in the UI test — this does NOT force dark on the iOS 26.5 simulator, producing a false-green (test passed even with the pin removed). Replaced with running the test on a simulator set to Dark appearance via 'xcrun simctl ui <sim> appearance dark', which gives a genuine red (pin off) / green (pin on).
Fix that worked: .preferredColorScheme(.light) at the WindowGroup root (propagates to sheets) + explicit .foregroundStyle(Theme.ink) on all fields as belt-and-suspenders.

features.description: Add an iOS app icon (Warm Editorial sunrise: terracotta half-sun rising over a sage horizon on a warm dawn gradient), 1024x1024 opaque, wired via Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset.
features.coverage: n/a (asset; verified by build compiling AppIcon into Assets.car + Info.plist CFBundleIcons)
features.notes: Icon designed as HTML rendered at 1024 via Playwright, flattened opaque. Xcode auto-generates all sizes from the single 1024 source. Installed and verified on the XR.
approaches_tried: Rendered icon design via headless browser at exact 1024x1024; first composition had rays hidden behind the horizon band, fixed layering (rays behind sun, ground clips sun bottom).
